emails.subject: FW: remote offices
emails.body: Under commitments to PEC, Enron has commited to provide office, computers, trading systems, etc. If this is changing, I need to know quickly. I have been provided indications from several outside counterparties, some divisions within Newco and I thought Netco as holding interest in purchasing the Chicago business. Due to the obvious indication of value for the joint venture with PEC, I believe it imperative that the business/office be operational until March, 2002, at a minimum. Please advise. Laura Luce

emails.subject: RE: remote offices
emails.body: I do not understand the Netco/Newco comments. Clearly at this point Enron the estate should maintain the space until it is sold to a new buyer. That would be a Don Miller/Fallon final call but I think its a no brainer.

emails.subject: RE: remote offices
emails.body: Thank you. I will ensure we get with Don Miller. Laura
